angle_summary: a Thai Noodle Bar has built one of the strongest technical restaurant websites in the region, and the next logical step is making the business's excellent reputation more visible at the point where visitors decide to book. Surfacing existing Google reviews on-site and adding lightweight local content can convert more of the traffic the site already earns, without changing anything that is working.
proof_points: Google review rating of 4.6 across 102 reviews is a strong and verified trust asset that is not currently confirmed to appear anywhere on the website itself.
Mobile PageSpeed of 97 and desktop of 99 indicate the site is already technically optimised, meaning any additions can be implemented cleanly without disrupting existing performance.
No active blog or updates section is present, leaving a gap in locally relevant, indexable content that could capture seasonal and tourism-driven search queries in Westport.
recommended_offer_type: Quick win package (fixed scope, 7 to 14 days)
offer_scope_points: Embed or surface two to three curated Google review excerpts with star ratings on the homepage and near the booking CTA to reinforce trust for first-time visitors.
Implement and verify local Restaurant schema markup including address, opening hours, cuisine type, and phone number to support local search visibility for Westport dining queries.
Set up a lightweight content section for seasonal menus or monthly updates, with a simple template the business can update independently or hand off for managed support.
personalisation_notes: The business has 102 Google reviews averaging 4.6 stars, which is a genuinely strong reputation signal for a restaurant in Westport and a natural hook for outreach that feels specific rather than generic.
Westport is a seasonal tourism destination, meaning there is a timely angle around capturing visitor search traffic during peak season that gives the outreach natural urgency without being alarmist.
suggested_cta: If it would be useful, I am happy to send over a one-page summary of what we would do and why, or we could have a quick ten-minute call this week to talk through it.
avoid_saying: Avoid suggesting the website has problems or needs fixing, as the business is likely proud of the site and any implication it is underperforming will trigger defensiveness.
Avoid mentioning the need for regular blog posts or content writing, as this is a known objection and will raise concerns about time commitment before the conversation has begun.
unknowns_to_confirm: Whether Google reviews are currently featured anywhere on the website, or only visible externally on Google Maps, as this determines how much of the quick win scope is already in place.
Whether there is an existing maintenance or hosting support arrangement in place, as this affects whether a care plan conversation is premature or a natural next step after the quick win.
